summary: Your code incorrectly skips pairs when numbers are equal, which can miss valid sums like in the case of [3,3] with target 6. The nested loop structure is good, but you should remove the unnecessary condition that checks for equality. Always ensure your logic handles all possible cases without excluding

review_items.issue: The code incorrectly skips pairs when numbers are equal, even if they sum to target
review_items.fix_suggestion: Look at the condition that skips pairs when nums[i] equals nums[j]. In the failing test case with nums = [3,3] and target = 6, when i=0 and j=1, both numbers are equal, so the continue statement skips checking their sum. However, the problem allows using two different indices even if the numbers have the same value, as long as they are not the same element. The inner loop already ensures j starts at i+1, so you're never using the same element twice. Consider whether this condition is necessary. Try removing or adjusting it to allow checking the sum even when the numbers are equal.
